Author: chaask
Content: Does the wafer.space GF180 PDK have a Virtuoso compatible version. I am trying to do some analog design work, and i am comfortable inside Virtuoso. So just wondering, if we have that option.

Author: nmz787
Content: the only DRC seems to be klayout

Author: 246tnt
Content: Wouldn't that be distributed by Cadence directly ?

Author: 246tnt
Content: I know for sky130 open pdk, Cadence is the one ditributing the matching PDK.

Author: 246tnt
Content: It's still subject to NDA and such so no-one else can distribute it ...

Author: chaask
Content: I dont think cadence version is opensource. but generally mpw vendors or the foundry supply the closed source pdk under nda. is that possible with us.

Author: chaask
Content: will our mpw partner also be able do that @Tim 'mithro' Ansell .

Author: mithro_
Content:
wafer.space doesn't care how you create the GDS but we don't supply access to any proprietary PDKs.

Your GDS also needs to be 100% clean by the wafer.space platform, not whatever the cadence or any proprietary PDK says. 

Wafer.space also does not support waivers from customers.

Author: mithro_
Content:
Some people have access to a mostly compatible Cadence PDK through agreements with other entities and have successfully taped out.

As well, GF will *not* respond to requests for the PDK without other relationships with them already.

Author: mithro_
Content: I'm unclear if Cadence tooling is able to load the open source file formats. They are old enough / simple enough they might work with a bit of work.

Author: mithro_
Content:
Btw wafer.space also doesn't provide any direct support for tooling - open or closed.

If you need technical help in creating designs (with whatever tooling), the design help page has a number of companies willing to provide paid support services.

Author: nmz787
Content: my experience also is that the foundry provides the PDK, with support for any tools they expect users will want. I have never heard of tool vendors supplying a foundry PDK component

Author: nmz787
Content: for something like DRC, I don't think this is possible without some translator which surely Cadence/Synopsys isn't making for working with klayout

Author: nmz787
Content: OASIS and GDS are industry standards, so of course closed-source tools support them

Author: nmz787
Content: APR techfiles are mostly ASCII with grid definitions (pitches, CDs, spaces) so a translator is likely easier to code (I suppose even for AI)
